turns.user_message: I keep getting this weird tightness right in the center of my chest that just shows up out of nowhere. It started happening a couple of weeks ago and honestly it catches me completely off guard every single time. My breathing gets really shallow and I have to consciously remind myself to take slower breaths just to feel normal again. It usually fades after a few minutes but leaves me feeling totally drained and a little rattled for the rest of the day. I really wish I knew what triggers it because it feels so random and exhausting to deal with.

That same heavy pressure in my chest keeps returning and it is starting to feel like a constant background noise I cannot tune out. I notice it popping up multiple times a day now, even when I am just sitting quietly trying to relax. My hands get clammy and my heart starts pounding so fast that I have to pause whatever I am doing just to catch my breath. It is really frustrating because I feel like I am losing my grip on my own routine and my mind keeps jumping to the worst possible outcomes. I just want it to stop interrupting my thoughts so I can actually focus on anything else.

The physical sensation has completely taken over and now I feel like my entire body is vibrating with this relentless, suffocating energy. Every single breath feels like a struggle and my chest gets so constricted that I genuinely panic about whether I will ever catch my breath again. I try to push through it but the intensity just keeps building until I am completely paralyzed by this overwhelming wave of dread. My thoughts are racing a million miles an hour and I feel completely disconnected from everything around me while trapped inside my own head. It is exhausting to exist like this when every moment feels like I am bracing for something terrible to happen.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This just started recently.
